frameworks.name: population receptive field (pRF) mapping
frameworks.one_line: Models spatial tuning of voxels to characterize visual field coverage across face areas.

frameworks.name: dual-stream face processing (ventral vs lateral)
frameworks.one_line: Functional dissociation of static vs dynamic face streams extended to spatial-computation differences.

frameworks.name: structural-functional scaffolding via white-matter connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Diffusion-MRI tracts from EVC shape spatial tuning of high-level face regions.

core_question: Do ventral and lateral face-selective regions perform different spatial computations, and are these grounded in white-matter connections to early visual cortex?
key_assumptions: pRF properties reflect feedforward spatial sampling of high-level regions.
White-matter endpoints in EVC indicate the eccentricity origin of inputs.
manipulations: Modality (functional pRF mapping vs diffusion MRI tractography).
key_findings: Ventral face-selective pRFs concentrate near the foveal center of gaze.
Lateral face-selective pRFs extend into peripheral visual field.
Ventral regions connect predominantly with foveal EVC via white matter.
Lateral regions show more uniform white-matter connectivity across EVC eccentricities.
interpretations: Spatial computations differ across ventral and lateral face streams.
Fine-grained white-matter patterning from EVC scaffolds spatial tuning in high-level cortex.
